items.title: Pomerantz Law Firm Announces the Filing of a Class Action Against AeroVironment, Inc. and Certain Officers – AVAV
items.summary: Pomerantz LLP filed a class action lawsuit against AeroVironment and certain officers for alleged securities fraud. The lawsuit claims defendants made false statements about the SCAR program's growth prospects while understating competition risks. AeroVironment's stock fell significantly following a government stop work order in January 2026, contract termination in March 2026, and the Space Force's decision to diversify suppliers and use commercial off-the-shelf solutions instead of a single-vendor bespoke solution.

items.title: AEROVIRONMENT DEADLINE: ROSEN, TRUSTED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages AeroVironment, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - AVAV
items.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against AeroVironment, Inc. (AVAV) alleging the company made false and misleading statements regarding competition for U.S. Space Force contracts (SCAR and SCN programs). Investors who purchased securities between June 25, 2025 and March 10, 2026 may be eligible for compensation. The lead plaintiff deadline is July 27, 2026.

items.title: AVAV DEADLINE: ROSEN, TRUSTED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages AeroVironment, Inc. Investors with Losses in Excess of $100K to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - AVAV
items.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against AeroVironment, Inc. for allegedly making false and misleading statements about competition risks related to U.S. Space Force contracts (SCAR and SCN programs). Investors who purchased securities between June 25, 2025 and March 10, 2026 may be eligible for compensation. The lead plaintiff deadline is July 27, 2026.

items.title: AeroVironment, Inc. (AVAV) Investors with Substantial Losses Have Opportunity to Lead Class Action Lawsuit, Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP Announces
items.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against AeroVironment for allegedly making false statements about its business prospects related to the U.S. Space Force's SCAR program. After a stop work order in January 2026 and subsequent contract termination in March 2026, the company's stock fell significantly and it reported a $151.3 million goodwill impairment. Investors with substantial losses can seek appointment as lead plaintiff by July 27, 2026.

items.title: AVAV Lead Plaintiff Deadline Approaching: Robbins LLP Urges Stockholders to Contact the Firm for Information in Advance of the July 27, 2026 Deadline
items.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against AeroVironment, Inc. for allegedly misleading investors about the viability of its SCAR program involvement. The company faced a U.S. government stop work order in January 2026 and subsequent contract termination in March 2026, resulting in a $151.3 million goodwill impairment and significant stock price declines. The lead plaintiff deadline is July 27, 2026.

items.title: Pomerantz Law Firm Announces the Filing of a Class Action Against AeroVironment, Inc.and Certain Officers – AVAV
items.summary: Pomerantz LLP filed a class action lawsuit against AeroVironment and certain officers for alleged securities fraud. The lawsuit covers investors who purchased AeroVironment securities between June 25, 2025 and March 10, 2026. Defendants allegedly made false statements about the SCAR program's growth prospects while understating competition risks. The U.S. Space Force issued a stop work order in January 2026, terminated the contract in March 2026, and subsequently announced plans to diversify suppliers using commercial off-the-shelf solutions instead of a single-vendor bespoke solution, causing significant stock price declines.

items.title: AVAV DEADLINE: ROSEN, TRUSTED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages AeroVironment, Inc. Investors with Losses in Excess of $100K to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - AVAV
items.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against AeroVironment, Inc. for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding competition for U.S. Space Force contracts (SCAR and SCN programs). The lawsuit claims the company overstated its business prospects and understated competitive threats. Investors with losses exceeding $100K are encouraged to join the class action before the July 27, 2026 lead plaintiff deadline.

items.title: AVAV FINAL DEADLINE: ROSEN, A LONGSTANDING LAW FIRM, Encourages AeroVironment, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - AVAV
items.summary: Rosen Law Firm is reminding AeroVironment, Inc. investors of the July 27, 2026 lead plaintiff deadline for a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that AeroVironment made false and misleading statements regarding competition for U.S. Space Force contracts (SCAR and SCN programs), overstating its business prospects. Investors who purchased securities between June 25, 2025 and March 10, 2026 may be eligible for compensation.

items.title: INVESTOR DEADLINE: Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP Announces that AeroVironment, Inc. (AVAV) Investors with Substantial Losses Have Opportunity to Lead Class Action Lawsuit
items.summary: Law firm Robbins Geller announces a class action lawsuit against AeroVironment for allegedly making false statements about its SCAR program prospects. The company allegedly understated competition risks and overstated business prospects. After a U.S. Space Force stop work order in January 2026 and subsequent contract termination in March 2026, AeroVironment stock fell significantly and the company reported a $151.3 million goodwill impairment. Investors with substantial losses can seek lead plaintiff status by July 27, 2026.

items.title: AVAV DEADLINE: ROSEN, A LONGSTANDING FIRM, Encourages AeroVironment, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - AVAV
items.summary: Rosen Law Firm reminds AeroVironment investors who purchased securities between June 25, 2025 and March 10, 2026 of the July 27, 2026 lead plaintiff deadline in an ongoing securities class action. The lawsuit alleges AeroVironment made false statements regarding competition risks for U.S. Space Force contracts (SCAR and SCN programs), overstating business prospects and misleading investors.

items.title: $AVAV Stock News: AeroVironment Stock Dropped 17% after Goodwill Impairment Disclosed – Investors Notified to Contact BFA Law about the Securities Class Action Lawsuit - GlobeNewswire

items.title: AVAV INVESTOR ALERT: Holzer & Holzer, LLC Reminds Investors of the July 27, 2026 Lead Plaintiff Deadline in the AeroVironment, Inc. Securities Class Action
items.summary: Holzer & Holzer, LLC has filed shareholder class action lawsuits against AeroVironment, Inc., alleging false statements regarding competition for satellite communication work. The firm is also investigating Primoris Services Corporation and has filed a lawsuit against Black Rock Coffee Bar, Inc. Investors who suffered losses during specified periods are encouraged to contact the firm before the July 27, 2026 lead plaintiff deadline.
